focus: H-56-1 cascade consistency across the 16-folder sweep

graphEvents.name: H-56-1 cascade ordering consistency
T-CNS-03 was recorded as two batches across 17 enumerated folders, not one uniform 16-folder sweep
Seven historical sweep folders now have description timestamps more than 10 minutes older than graph last_save_at
Packet language disagrees on whether continuity freshness is one-sided or symmetric

focus: Q2: description.json auto-regeneration preserve-field gaps

graphEvents.name: Root causes and mitigation options for description.json auto-regeneration preserve-field gaps
generate-description.ts rewrites description.json from generated objects and only carries forward memorySequence plus memoryNameHistory in explicit-description mode
Phase 026 sibling folders 007 008 009 010 014 015 and 017 currently share a stub-shaped description.json with empty scope keyFiles tags and implementationSummary
Target sibling graph-metadata files leave derived.last_description_sync_at null so graph freshness can advance without durable proof of a same-pass description refresh

focus: Q3: code-graph readiness vocabulary completeness

graphEvents.name: Reachability of SharedPayloadTrustState across the 7 code-graph handlers
readiness-contract projects GraphFreshness onto a 3-state trust subset live stale absent despite the canonical 8-state SharedPayloadTrustState type
Handler reachability is asymmetric: query status and scan emit only readiness-contract values, context adds unavailable on probe crash, and the three ccc handlers emit unavailable only
cached imported rebuilt and rehomed are unreachable from the 7 code-graph handlers; imported rebuilt and rehomed appear documentation-only in the inspected mcp_server runtime

focus: Q9: evidence-marker bracket-depth lint false-positive surface

graphEvents.name: False-positive surface of evidence-marker bracket-depth lint under markdown edge cases
Bracket-depth parsing ignores parentheses and shipped tests confirm parenthesis-heavy evidence content closes safely when the closing bracket is present
Fence recognition is narrower than valid markdown because the parser only toggles on column-0 triple backticks and does not track longer outer-fence width
No shipped unit tests cover indented fences or four-backtick outer fences containing inner triple-backtick content

focus: Q10: continuity-freshness threshold calibration

graphEvents.name: Calibration basis for the 10-minute continuity freshness threshold
continuity-freshness.ts enforces a one-sided stale-after-graph-save budget and treats negative deltas as benign clock drift
Commit 32a180bba introduces the 10-minute threshold with synthetic 9:59 pass and 10:01 warn fixtures but no checked-in latency calibration evidence
Repo-wide sampled continuity-vs-graph timestamp pairs show zero positive deltas within 10 minutes, reinforcing that the threshold is not visibly grounded in observed on-disk latency behavior

focus: Q5: retry-budget policy calibration

graphEvents.name: Calibration basis for the partial_causal_link_unresolved retry budget
retry-budget.ts hardcodes MAX_RETRIES=3 and checked-in docs justify it as bounded hot-loop control rather than an empirically measured success-rate threshold
The shipped retry-budget and post-insert tests prove first-three-then-stop behavior but provide no real attempt-2 or attempt-3 resolution distribution
Repo-wide occurrences of partial_causal_link_unresolved are dominated by code, tests, and docs, leaving no checked-in runtime histogram for calibration

focus: Q6: AsyncLocalStorage caller-context propagation edge cases

graphEvents.name: Whether AsyncLocalStorage caller-context survives common and detached async boundaries without silent null fallback
The shipped caller-context path is explicitly tested across await setTimeout nested runs and Promise.all, and the only live consumer reads context at session-resume handler entry
Coverage does not yet prove setImmediate queueMicrotask watcher or detached callback handoffs, leaving a future-consumer contract gap rather than a demonstrated current regression

getCallerContext still returns null silently and session-resume tolerates null-session transports, so missing binding can degrade into a permissive-looking path unless consumers assert context explicitly

graphEvents.name: Remaining Copilot-specific observability and hardening gaps after Cluster E
Copilot bootstrap support is still contradictory between runtime_capabilities.json hookBootstrap false and capability_matrix.md hook-bootstrap yes claims
Phase 019 documents an ARG_MAX-safe @path fallback for cli-copilot, but the shipped YAML branches and cli-matrix tests implement only positional copilot -p command substitution
cli-copilot still relies on out-of-band ~/.copilot/config.json for reasoning intensity, and executor audit cannot verify that effective runtime setting

focus: Q9: Evidence-marker bracket-depth lint false positives

graphEvents.name: Evidence-marker bracket-depth lint false-positive surface
Indented triple-backtick fences are not recognized as code context so example evidence markers inside them are reported as malformed
A nested line-start triple-backtick inside an open fence prematurely flips fenced-code mode and exposes example text to marker parsing
Delimiter-heavy prose with nested brackets and parens still parses as ok so prose-level bracket depth is not the active false-positive driver

focus: Q10: continuity-freshness threshold calibration

graphEvents.name: Calibration basis for the 10-minute continuity freshness threshold
continuity-freshness encodes a fixed 10 minute stale budget without any checked-in telemetry or calibration hook
shipped tests validate 9:59 pass and 10:01 warn boundaries but do not measure real canonical-save latency
checked-in spec folders show no positive continuity-vs-graph deltas inside 0 to 10 minutes; observed positive deltas are hours to days old while active folders skew negative

focus: Q1: two-batch 16-folder metadata refresh ordering invariants

graphEvents.name: Whether the two-batch 16-folder metadata refresh introduced ordering inconsistencies between description and graph metadata
Phase 017 freshness closure was a two-mechanism rollout: 7 folders refreshed by H-56-1 canonical-save cascade and 11 by a later description-only jq sweep
Mixed polarity is structural because canonical save writes description first then graph, while standalone tools can refresh either side independently
No last_description_sync_at or equivalent provenance field exists to prove same-pass refresh between description and graph metadata

focus: Q2: preserve-field analysis for generate-description.js

graphEvents.name: Root causes and mitigation options for generate-description.js overwriting hand-authored rich content
generate-description regenerates a fresh minimal PerFolderDescription and only preserves memorySequence plus memoryNameHistory, so authored description keys outside that contract are dropped on save
folder-discovery stale-description repair routes existing files through the same full-object regeneration path, making rich metadata loss reachable during routine maintenance

The lowest-blast-radius mitigation is a regen helper that overwrites only canonical generated fields while preserving unknown authored keys and temporarily disables stale rich-file auto-repair until that helper ships

focus: Q3: audit the 7 code-graph sibling handlers against the 8 SharedPayloadTrustState values and determine whether the readiness vocabulary is fully reachable or partly dead-code/documentation-only

graphEvents.name: Whether all 8 SharedPayloadTrustState values are reachable from the 7 code-graph sibling handlers
The seven code-graph handlers only emit a four-state subset of the canonical trust-state union: live, stale, absent, and unavailable
cached remains active on compact-cache and hook surfaces but is not reachable from any of the code-graph sibling handlers
imported, rebuilt, and rehomed appear to be retained compatibility vocabulary with no active producer in the audited code-graph handler surface

focus: Q9: refine the evidence-marker bracket-depth lint audit around nested fences, mismatched fences, and paren-heavy markdown so Phase 019 can separate true false-positive risks from adjacent parser failure modes

graphEvents.name: Four-backtick fences are accidentally safe under the current triple-backtick prefix check and did not reproduce a marker false positive in this pass
Mismatched or unclosed fences suppress later evidence markers instead of producing additional false-positive lint hits, making them a false-negative auditability gap
Indented triple-backtick fences remain the confirmed live false-positive path because example markers inside them are parsed as malformed prose

focus: Q5 refinement: quantify the checked-in failure-resolution distribution behind the partial_causal_link_unresolved retry budget

graphEvents.name: The checked-in retry-budget corpus encodes only a synthetic 3-then-stop boundary: attempts 0-2 remain retryable, failure 3 exhausts the budget, and there are no mixed unresolved-to-resolved examples
No checked-in runtime logs or JSONL traces capture real partial_causal_link_unresolved attempt outcomes, so the threshold cannot be calibrated from artifact mining alone

focus: Q6: inspect whether AsyncLocalStorage caller-context propagation survives common async boundaries such as setTimeout, Promise.all, and deferred event-loop handoffs, or whether some Phase 017/018 paths still silently drop getCallerContext()

graphEvents.name: Whether AsyncLocalStorage caller-context propagation survives the async boundaries used by the shipped Phase 017/018 surface

The current shipped caller-context surface is effectively one synchronous session_resume read inside the transport wrapper, and repo tests plus a live runtime probe show ALS survives await, setTimeout, Promise.all, queueMicrotask, setImmediate, and timers/promises handoffs for that path

Coverage remains thinner than the runtime probe because checked-in tests do not yet assert setImmediate or queueMicrotask, and post-dispatch callbacks execute outside the current runWithCallerContext wrapper even though they do not presently read caller context

focus: Q7: inspect the remaining Copilot-specific observability and autonomous-execution hardening gaps after Cluster E, especially whether any executor-specific callback or recovery path widens the current transport-context surface without comparable tests.

The shipped Copilot hook and compact-cache tests cover wrapper wiring and happy-path cached recovery, but do not reproduce a current transport-context widening bug
Post-dispatch validation accepts non-native iteration records without executor attribution, leaving Copilot recovery observability weaker than the documented audit contract
Copilot-specific recovery coverage remains happy-path biased and does not cover autonomous delegated or degraded recovery flows

focus: Stay on Q7 for one tighter pass: inspect the actual cli-copilot executor implementation and any reducer or audit code that writes iteration JSONL so we can confirm whether executor attribution and recovery metadata survive failure, stuck-recovery, and partial-write cases in code rather than in documentation alone.

Q10 refinement: re-check whether the 10-minute continuity-freshness budget reflects any real same-save latency pattern in the live 026 corpus, or whether it still behaves like a coarse policy guardrail that mostly masks stale and format-skewed packets.

The validator still passes hours-scale negative deltas as clock_drift, proving the freshness rule is one-sided and advisory rather than a same-pass equivalence invariant

focus: Shift to Q1 and inspect the 16-folder canonical-save sweep ordering invariants directly.
